title: Intensely Smoky Croatian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Croatian dish featuring tender lamb and a rich, smoky flavor, served in a scallop shell. This macrobiotic-friendly recipe is perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions: Heat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the lamb cubes with the caster sugar, smoked paprika, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
In a large Dutch oven, cook the lamb over medium-high heat until it is browned on all sides.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the lamb broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the stew is hot and bubbly.
Serve the stew in scallop shells, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme.
